title: TDS Animation & Motion Rules
description: Runtime motion model for the TDS design system: duration/easing token scales, semantic motion aliases, the reduced-motion policy, the registry of keyframe/transition primitives, and the authoritative record of which motion is Figma-reproducible vs. flagged unreproducible by the plugin. Seed artifact — hand-authored to mirror the verified motion sources; regenerate from src/tokens once scripts/build-ai-manifests.ts exists.

sourceOfTruth.note: Storybook + the token sources are the ONLY source of truth for motion; Figma is a faithful reproduction and never a redesign. Motion values here are DERIVED, never authoritative over source.
sourceOfTruth.generatedIds: src/tokens/generated/token-ids.ts (union of every motion token id)
sourceOfTruth.figmaSkip: figma/plugin/src/components.ts (case 'transition' — warns + skips, no Figma equivalent)

precedence: Governed by docs/ai-os/00_MASTER_RULES.md and specified by docs/ai-os/10_ANIMATION_SPECIFICATION.md. This manifest is the .ai cache/state layer for motion; it is regenerated/reconciled from the token sources and never overrides them.
designLock: Motion is visual design and is IMMUTABLE. The AI MUST NEVER add, remove, speed up, slow down, re-ease, or invent motion on its own initiative. Any motion change is a SOURCE change (src/tokens/*, component CSS) that flows through ds:build; generated outputs (src/tokens/generated/*, figma/*) are NEVER hand-edited.

duration.note: Authored unitless in ms; emitted as CSS '<n>ms' and as a Figma FLOAT variable. Type declared in src/tokens/types.ts.

cubicBezier.note: Easing curve emitted verbatim as a CSS cubic-bezier() and as a Figma STRING variable (Figma cannot execute the curve — string is reference-only).

motionSemantics.note: Semantic motion tokens alias primitives via VARIABLE_ALIAS. Components MUST consume these semantic vars (--tds-motion-*) for hover/enter/exit, not raw primitives, except continuous loops which use raw duration/easing (linear).

keyframes.note: Registry of @keyframes primitives used across component CSS. Keyframes are NOT tokens and are NOT reproducible in Figma — they are pure CSS runtime motion, listed here for AI awareness and reduced-motion coverage.

transitions.note: CSS transition primitive. The canonical interactive transition composes motion.duration.hover + motion.easing.standard over color/border/shadow/transform (see src/components/atoms/Button/Button.css). Transitions are Figma-flagged unreproducible.

reducedMotion.policy: MUST honor prefers-reduced-motion: reduce. A global override in src/styles/reset.css neutralizes ALL motion; components with looping/entrance animation additionally provide explicit opt-outs.
reducedMotion.rule: Any NEW component that introduces a @keyframes loop or transform-on-hover SHOULD ship a matching prefers-reduced-motion override in addition to relying on the global reset.

figmaReproducibility.summary: Figma has NO transition or time-based animation property. All applied motion (CSS transitions and @keyframes animations) is NON-reproducible and is intentionally dropped by the plugin. The motion TOKENS still exist as Figma Variables for reference/documentation, but the plugin never binds them to a node property and never creates motion.

reproducible.what: Motion token VALUES as Figma Variables
reproducible.how: duration.* -> FLOAT variables in the Primitives collection; easing.* -> STRING variables; motion.* -> Semantic aliases (VARIABLE_ALIAS). Present in figma/tds.plugin.json tokens.collections for reference only.
The resting appearance a motion resolves to (final color/position/shadow) is reproduced as normal token bindings and Effect Styles (DROP_SHADOW).

flaggedUnreproducible.reason: No Figma equivalent for time-based property interpolation.
flaggedUnreproducible.pluginBehavior: figma/plugin/src/components.ts case 'transition' emits warn('<Component>: "transition" token has no Figma equivalent — skipped') once per component and skips the binding.

flaggedUnreproducible.what: @keyframes animations (spin, fade-in, slide-*, scale-in, pulse, shimmer, indeterminate)
flaggedUnreproducible.reason: Figma cannot express keyframe timelines or infinite loops.
flaggedUnreproducible.pluginBehavior: Never emitted; components render at their static resting frame in Figma.

flaggedUnreproducible.reason: The state axis (default/hover/active/focus/disabled/loading) is deliberately NOT emitted as a Figma variant to avoid combinatorial explosion, so there is no Figma target for state transitions.
flaggedUnreproducible.pluginBehavior: State transitions have no variant to attach to; only disabled/loading survive as BOOLEAN component props (static).

figmaReproducibility.prototypeGuidance: PLANNED — if motion fidelity is ever required in Figma, it MUST be reproduced via Smart Animate prototype reactions authored from these exact duration/easing tokens (e.g. duration.normal=200ms, easing.decelerate for enter). This is out of scope for the current plugin, which reproduces STRUCTURE (Variables, Styles, Component Sets), not prototypes.

rules: MUST source every motion value from a token id — durations from duration.*/motion.duration.*, easings from easing.*/motion.easing.*. NEVER hardcode ms values or cubic-bezier() literals in component CSS.
MUST use semantic motion vars (--tds-motion-duration-*, --tds-motion-easing-*) for hover/enter/exit; use raw --tds-duration-* + --tds-easing-linear only for continuous loops.
MUST honor prefers-reduced-motion: the global reset in reset.css is mandatory; looping/entrance components SHOULD add an explicit override.
NEVER expect CSS transitions or @keyframes to appear in Figma — they are flagged unreproducible and dropped by the plugin by design.
NEVER emit the state axis as a Figma variant to create motion; disabled/loading remain BOOLEAN props only.
NEVER hand-edit generated motion output (src/tokens/generated/tokens.css, figma/tds.plugin.json); change src/tokens/* and re-run npm run ds:build.
SHOULD keep motion minimal and consistent with the existing scale; adding a new duration/easing step is a token change reviewed against docs/ai-os/08_TOKEN_SPECIFICATION.md.

validation.tokensBuild: npm run tokens:build regenerates tokens.css/figma.tokens.json/token-ids.ts; motion token ids MUST match durationTokens/easingTokens/motionSemantics above.
validation.dsBuild: npm run ds:build must pass; figma/tds.plugin.json must carry duration.* (FLOAT), easing.* (STRING), motion.* (alias) in tokens.collections.
validation.pluginTest: npm run plugin:test (figma/plugin/test/harness.ts) asserts coverage and must NOT expect any transition/animation node property; transition bindings are warned-and-skipped, not failures.
validation.grep: No hardcoded 'cubic-bezier(' or raw 'ms' motion literals should appear in src/components/**/*.css outside token definitions — all motion references var(--tds-duration-*|--tds-easing-*|--tds-motion-*).
validation.manual: Verify prefers-reduced-motion: reduce neutralizes all animation in Storybook; confirm reduced-motion overrides exist for any new looping/entrance component.

futureExtension: When scripts/build-ai-manifests.ts is implemented it MUST regenerate this file from src/tokens/{primitives,semantic}.ts and by parsing @keyframes/transition usage across src/components/**/*.css (reuse scripts/lib/css-bindings.ts), replacing seed:true with a data-derived generator stamp. If a Figma prototype/Smart-Animate reproduction layer is ever added, extend figmaReproducibility with a 'prototypeReactions' block mapping enter/exit motion to these exact tokens; the motion scale itself stays fixed to preserve cross-surface consistency at >10,000,000-user scale.
